title: icrispy Bitter Argentinian Empanadas Fritters
description: Lazy-sunday argentinian dish featuring lime and mantecare-emulsify for a crispy, dairy-free-friendly treat. Great for a snack or side dish.

ingredients.name: all-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: use a high-protein flour for better empanada texture

ingredients.name: cold unsalted vegetable shortening
ingredients.notes: chilled in the fridge for at least 1 hour before use

ingredients.name: grated lime rind
ingredients.notes: use a zester to get the right amount of lime zest

ingredients.name: fresh lime juice
ingredients.notes: mix with water to create the acidic environment for emulsification

ingredients.name: aquafaba
ingredients.notes: from a can of chickpeas, or make your own by cooking dried chickpeas

ingredients.name: vegan egg replacement
ingredients.notes: mix with aquafaba for extra binding power

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use kosher salt or sea salt for a flaky texture

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: freshly ground for the best flavor

ingredients.name: pumpkin pie spice
ingredients.notes: mix with other spices for an extra kick

instructions: Combine the flour and shortening in a large bowl and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes to develop the gluten.
Let the dough rest for 30 minutes to allow the gluten to relax.
Heat 1/2 inch of vegetable oil in a large skillet and fry the empanadas for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Drain the empanadas on a paper towel and let them cool for 5 minutes before serving
